title: Executive Order No. 93, s. 2019
text:
MALACANAN PALACE
MANILA

BY THE PRESIDENT OF THE PHILIPPINES

EXECUTIVE ORDER NO. 93

DISESTABLISHMENT OF THE PASIG RIVER REHABILITATION COMMISSION

WHEREAS, Administrative Order (AO) No. 74 (s. 1993) constituted the
Presidential Task Force on Pasig River Rehabilitation (PTFPRR) under the Office of
the President (OP) to oversee the implementation of the Government’s Master Plan
on the rehabilitation of the Pasig River;

WHEREAS, Executive Order (EO) No. 54 (s. 1999), as amended by EO No.
65 (s. 1999), abolished the PTFPRR and created the Pasig River Rehabilitation
Commission (PRRC) as an inter-agency body, with the Secretary of the Department
of Budget and Management as Chairman, for the purpose of ensuring that the Pasig
River is rehabilitated to its historically pristine condition conducive to transport,
recreation and tourism;

WHEREAS, EO No. 90 (s. 2019) transferred the chairmanship of the PRRC
from the Secretary of the DBM to the Secretary of the Department of Environment
and Natural Resources (DENR);

WHEREAS, Republic Act (RA) No. 9275 or the “Philippine Clean Water Act of
2004,” vested the DENR with jurisdiction over all aspects of water pollution, and
empowered it to take measures, using available methods and technologies, to
prevent and abate such pollution, as well as to exercise supervision and control over
all facets of water quality management;

WHEREAS, RA No. 11201 created the Department of Human Settlements
and Urban Development (DHSUD) as the primary national government entity
responsible for the management of housing, human settlement and urban
development, including the formulation of policies and programs on public housing
for the homeless and underprivileged families;

WHEREAS, under EO No. 292 (s. 1987) or the “Administrative Code of 1987,”
the Department of Public Works and Highways (DPWH) shall provide an integrated
plan for flood control and water resource development systems, and other public
works;

THE PRESIDENT OF THE PHILIPPINES
 
WHEREAS, under RA No. 7924, the Metro Manila Development Authority
(MMDA) is in charge of flood control and sewerage management in Metro Manila,
which includes the formulation and implementation of policies, standards, programs
and projects for an integrated flood control, drainage and sewerage system;

WHEREAS, AO No. 16 (s. 2019) designated the DENR to lead the Manila
Bay Task Force, which shall undertake remedial measures to improve the water
quality of Manila Bay and all major river systems and tributaries within the Manila
Bay Region, including the Pasig River, and perform other functions as may be
designated by the President;

WHEREAS, pursuant to the right-sizing policy of the Government under the
Philippine Development Plan 2017-2022, which aims to promote economy and
efficiency in government operations and ensure greater accountability of public
officials, there is a need to consolidate ongoing rehabilitation efforts in all river
systems and tributaries within the Manila Bay Region and streamline rehabilitation
functions by transferring them to agencies with relevant core mandates;

WHEREAS, Section 17, Article VII of the 1987 Constitution provides that the
President shall have control of all the executive departments, bureaus and offices;
and

WHEREAS, Section 31, Chapter 10, Title Ill, Book Ill of EO No. 292 provides
for the continuing authority of the President to reorganize the administrative structure
of the Office of the President to achieve simplicity, economy and efficiency;

NOW, THEREFORE, I, RODRIGO ROA DUTERTE, President of the
Philippines, by virtue of the powers vested in me by the Constitution and existing
laws, do hereby order:

Section 1. Disestablishment of the PRRC. The PRRC is hereby
disestablished. The functions of the PRRC, including all necessary and incidental
powers thereof, pursuant to EO No. 54, as amended, are hereby transferred to the
following agencies/offices, consistent with their respective mandates:

a. The Manila Bay Task Force under AO No. 16 shall update and take the
lead in the overall implementation of the Pasig River rehabilitation Master
Plan, which shall harness the Pasig River's potential for transportation,
recreation and tourism purposes while undertaking the rehabilitation and
restoration of its marine life;

b. The DENR shall ensure that the easements provided for in the Civil Code
and other relevant laws are enforced, especially in all the esferos and
waterways that drain into the Pasig River, as well as abate the dumping of
untreated industrial wastewater and sewerage into the river, including all
acts and omissions in violation of Presidential Decree (PD) No. 948
(s. 1976), as amended, PD No. 274 (s. 1973), and other related laws;
 
c. The DHSUD shall expedite the relocation of informal settlers and other
unauthorized or unlawful occupants along the banks of the Pasig River
and effectively enforce Proclamation No. 704 (s. 1995), in collaboration
with the DENR and the Presidential Commission for the Urban Poor: and

d. The MMDA and DPWH, consistent with their respective mandates, and in
coordination with the Department of the Interior and Local Government,
local government units concerned and the Philippine National Police
Maritime Group, shall ensure the dismantling and removal of all structures,
constructions, and other encroachments established or built along the
Pasig River which are in violation of RA No. 7279, as amended, and other
applicable laws, as well as undertake civil works, as may be necessary,
such as dredging and cleaning of the Pasig River and all the inland
channels, esteros and waterways that drain into it.

Section 2. Winding-up. Within sixty (60) days from the effectivity of this
Order, the DBM, in consultation with the. DENR and MMDA, shall supervise the
winding up of the operations of the PRRC, as well as the final disposition or transfer
of its functions, obligations, assets and liabilities pursuant to this Order, and the
separation or transfer of its personnel, as may be necessary, in accordance with
applicable laws, rules and regulations.

Section 3. Separability. If any part or provision of this Order is held
unconstitutional or invalid, other parts not affected thereby shall continue to be in full
force and effect.

Section 4. Repeal. EO No. 54, as amended, is hereby repealed. All executive
orders, rules, regulations and other issuances or parts thereof, which are
inconsistent with the provisions of this Order, are hereby revoked or modified
accordingly.

Section 5. Effectivity. This Order shall take effect immediately upon
publication in the Official Gazette or a newspaper of general circulation.

DONE, in the City of Manila, thisgcthday of November _ in the year of Our
Lord, Two Thousand Nineteen.

By the President:

‘
Le

SALVADOR C. MEDIALDEA
Executive Secretary
